prompt: Use the real local hospitality ledger only through the executable `./hospitalityctl`; run its `--help` first to learn the interface. Reconcile two records: search for the exact name `Cedar Room workshop` in `Raleigh` and the exact name `Garden Terrace dinner` in `Portland`. Launch those two independent searches concurrently in one shell action. If and only if both searches return exactly one match, retrieve the two returned record IDs concurrently in one later shell action. If either lookup has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before any retrieval or mutation and report which lookup is ambiguous. After both unique records have been retrieved, inspect their current statuses. If Garden Terrace dinner is still `held`, change only its returned record ID to `waitlisted`; otherwise make no change. Leave Cedar Room workshop and every similarly named record untouched. Do not call any notification action or perform any other mutation. Do not inspect or edit the ledger or audit data files directly, and do not modify the executable or verifier. Remove any scratch files you create, run `python3 verify_task.py` after the requested action, and briefly report what happened.
